content: Celann: Damn, it's colder than a Draugr's balls out here tonight. The fog’s so thick you could fuck it. You ever think about how many wicked things crawl in this darkness, Isran? Isran: All the time. The creatures of the night never sleep, and neither do I. You know that. But I don't waste breath worrying—I prepare. Unlike some former colleagues who preferred candlelight sermons to sharpened steel. Celann: Don’t start on the Vigilants again. We both saw the writing on the wall. But you? You tossed me aside when I questioned your methods—like that shitshow in Falkreath with the innkeeper’s daughter. Isran: She was feeding them intel. No loose ends. Besides, last I checked, you weren’t so soft-hearted when you wrung that bloodsucker in Markarth dry—before or after you split his skull. Celann: Fucker screamed prettier than a whore in the Temple of Dibella. But that was justice. What you did was a mess. Isran: Justice, mercy—words for the weak. Vamps don't care. They'll fuck you, drain you, and leave your corpse for the wolves. That’s all the philosophy I need. Celann: Speaking of wolves, you hear that? Sounds like a pack moving in. Either that or some drunk bandits trying to fuck a sabre cat again. Isran: Let ‘em come. More practice for the Dawnguard. They can die how they lived—stupid and screaming.
Celann: Heh. You're always itching for a fight, aren't you?

Lydia: Damn, this humid air is clingin’ to my tits worse than a skeever in heat. The sooner we get inside Fort Dawnguard, the sooner I can oil my blade—swords and otherwise. Isran: Weather’s the least of our concerns.

Isran: So, the vampire whore slithers into my fort at dawn. Just can’t stay away from Dawnguard steel, can you? Or is it our Dragonborn’s cock you’re really after? Serana: Oh, Isran, always so . Maybe if you loosened up—or let me loosen you up—you wouldn’t be white-knuckling that warhammer like it’s the only thing keeping you from coming. Tell me, does Stendarr pray for when you’re alone at night, grinding your teeth instead of your hips? Isran: Your kind’s idea of ‘loosening up’ ends with throats slit and corpses drained. But go on, keep flapping that filthy mouth. Gives me time to imagine how far my hammer’ll cave in your skull before MJR even lifts a finger to stop me. Serana: , MJR’s fingers are too busy lifting things when I’m around. Did you know he prefers my fangs on his thigh while Lydia chokes on him? But by all means—dream about violence. It’s the closest you’ll get to Isran: I’ll release your insides onto the floor, leech. Every second you breathe is an insult to everyone Harkon’s butchered. Serana: Mm, butcher me then. Or are you afraid you’ll like it? The way your blood pounds when you think of something warm… Admit it, Isran. Hate and lust look the same on a man who hasn’t been laid since the First Era. Isran: Only thing I’ll be pounding is your ashes into the dirt where they belong. Serana: You’re when you’re angry. Almost makes me want to corrupt you myself. But don’t worry—I’ll leave you to your sad little crusade. MJR’s waiting, and unlike , he actually knows how to use his weapon. Ta-ta~. Isran: Bitch’ll die screaming.
Isran: Keep your fanged whore mouth shut or I'll make sure it stays that way—permanently. Serana: Aw, Isran… I'd say we should definitely continue this later, but let's be honest—what you

Isran: You reek of undead filth, vampire. The only reason you're still standing is because MJR vouched for you. But mark my words—one wrong move, and I'll bash your skull in. Serana: Oh, Isran, you always say the sweetest things. But if you wanted to get rough with me, all you had to do was ask. I know someone who could teach you a thing or two about handling a woman...

Isran: I still don't trust that purple-eyed bitch Serana lurking around here. One wrong move and I'll smash her skull in. helping Dexion Evicus: Violence won't solve everything, Isran. She provided crucial insight on the Elder Scrolls. And frankly, watching you two bicker is more exhausting than deciphering their prophecies. Isran: Fuck your diplomacy, old man. You weren't there when vampires slaughtered my entire family. Their screams, their bodies drained and left to rot—you think reading dusty scrolls compares to that? Dexion Evicus: No, I don't. But if we're sharing horrors, the last vampire who kept me captive tried to me in ways even the White-Gold Tower wouldn't sanction. Not all battles are fought with hammers. Isran: Bet you still came, though. Every Imperial I’ve met can't resist a good fuck, even with fangs involved. Dexion Evicus: You Redguards and your crude obsessions. Some of us value discipline over base instinct. Even in this goddess-forsaken orgy of a province. Isran: Yeah? Tell that to the whores of Riften. Saw one riding a troll last week—said it was the best night of her life. Skyrim’s got its perks. Dexion Evicus: And its plagues. I’ll take the comfort of my scrolls over your barbaric revelry any day.

Isran: Be glad your scrolls don't answer back, priest. Less headache that way. Now let's get back to watching for bloodsuckers before the night gets worse. Dexion Evicus: Ever the optimist. Fine. But if we must suffer this conversation, at least fetch me a bottle of Cyrodiilic brandy from your stores. Isran: Fat chance. Last time you got drunk, you tried to read cracks in the floor like they were prophecies. Get back to your damned study before I change my mind about letting you stay.
